data.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (T5c — Security overlay). The overlay APPLIES: this plan adds real security surface (a public HTTPS review endpoint + Gerrit HTTP/SSH, an authn boundary on inbound webhooks, credentials/grants: a GitHub deploy key, a Gerrit bot HTTP token, an EC2 instance role + SSM secrets). Verdict: strong on 3 of 4 sub-checks; 2 grounded coaching findings (both on data-at-rest / secret-in-logs).

AFFIRMATIONS (T5c criteria this plan satisfies):
- (a) Endpoint access levels declared + sensitive paths gated: the receiver's access model is explicit and sound — nginx `/review/` on 443 is the only public surface; the stdio MCP is deliberately NOT exposed over HTTP; the webhook path is a two-layer control (PRIMARY internal-only container-to-container delivery over the private compose net with the receiver host-port loopback-bound + not in the SG; SECONDARY URL-embedded token, receiver-side token+source validation). The "webhooks plugin has NO HMAC" limitation is named honestly and the weight put on the network boundary rather than pretending a signature exists (Round-11 note + ADR-0014). Single-flight per (change_id, revision) + idempotency guard the review/vote critical section.
- (c) LEAST-PRIVILEGE on new grants: strong. The deploy key is single-repo WRITE-only to GitHub and is the sole ruleset bypass actor; the bot HTTP token's dual scope (REST + git-over-HTTPS read) is documented; the SSM read grant is scoped to the `/rebar/prod/*` prefix (explicitly NOT Resource:"*") with kms:Decrypt constrained by a kms:ViaService condition; the IAM-role ownership contract (S1 creates the role; S2/S4a only ADD separately-named scoped inline policies) prevents accidental over-grant/clobber. No wildcard admin-for-convenience.
- (d) Secret lifecycle (largely): SSM Parameter Store SecureString pulled to a `chmod 600` `.env` at boot; the bot token is rotatable (service-user.sh) and single-sourced; the enumerated SSM param set is completeness-checked (alert-endpoint + gerrit-bot-token) so no lookup fails on a missing param. No plaintext secrets committed to IaC.

FINDINGS (2):

- [ ] Specify encryption-at-rest (`encrypted = true` + a KMS key) for the dedicated `/var/gerrit` NoteDb EBS volume AND its DLM snapshots — the AC only names "a dedicated gp3 EBS volume ... with prevent_destroy + DLM daily snapshots" with no at-rest encryption, yet this volume holds `refs/meta/config`, which per ADR-0014 stores the live bot token (the webhook URL `?token=`). DLM snapshots inherit the source volume's encryption state, so an unencrypted volume yields unencrypted snapshots.
  - criteria: [T5c]
  - location: Decomposition & resolved design questions -> "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet; AC1 (dedicated gp3 EBS at /var/gerrit)
  - evidence: plan phrase "NoteDb on a dedicated gp3 EBS volume at `/var/gerrit` with `prevent_destroy` + DLM daily snapshots (S1)"; ABSENCE of any `encrypted`/KMS statement for that volume in the plan text (derisk note E1 dry-ran only the ROOT device as "gp3-encrypted", not the dedicated data volume); ADR-0014: the bot token lives in `refs/meta/config`, i.e. on this volume; T5c(b) encryption-at-rest where sensitive data is stored.
  - suggested_fix: Add to S1's volume AC: the /var/gerrit data volume is created with `encrypted = true` (AWS-managed or a project CMK), which propagates to the DLM snapshots; state it explicitly so the token-bearing refs/meta/config is encrypted at rest.

- [ ] Address the URL-token-in-logs leak vector: the bot token is passed as a `?token=<token>` query parameter on every webhook POST, and query strings are a classic secret-in-logs sink (nginx access log, the receiver's request log, Gerrit's webhooks-plugin delivery log). The plan/ADR-0014 bound the token's exposure in refs/meta/config and via replicatePermissions=false, but do NOT state that the token is redacted from access/request logs on the delivery path.
  - criteria: [T5c]
  - location: Round-11 remediation -> inbound-auth note; ADR-0014 "SECONDARY — URL-embedded token"
  - evidence: ADR-0014 phrase "http://review-bot:8000/webhook?token=<token>"; T5c(d) "no secret-logging"; ABSENCE of any log-redaction statement for the `?token=` query param on the nginx/receiver/webhooks-plugin log path.
  - suggested_fix: State the redaction posture — e.g. the receiver logs the request path with the token stripped/masked, nginx `/review/` is configured not to log query args (or logs a redacted URI), and if the webhooks-plugin logs the destination URL it is treated as a secret-bearing log; OR move the token to a header if the plugin supports one, keeping only the network-ACL primary gate in the URL.
